campbell.title: Campbell City Council — May 5, 2026
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review transit funding reports, approve a new policy about how city property can be used, and discuss efforts to fill open city jobs and keep current employees.
keyTopics: Santa Clara Valley Transportation Authority's investment report for March 2026
New policy restricting use of city property for federal civil enforcement
City job openings and plans to recruit and retain employees

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — April 15, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council held a public hearing about cleaning up hazardous conditions on properties identified by the county and voted to move forward with the cleanup work. They also adopted a new technology policy for the city and approved a process for residents to request accommodations when needed.
keyTopics: Public hearing on hazardous property abatement ordered by the county
Adoption of new Technology Disruption Policy
Process for residents to request reasonable accommodations

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos Town Council — May 5, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos Town Council met to approve routine meeting minutes and address fire safety and landscaping issues. The council declared overgrown brush and hazardous vegetation a public nuisance and approved an engineer's report for landscape and lighting improvements. They also authorized the Town Manager to sign multi-year service agreements with contractors.
keyTopics: Hazardous brush declared a public nuisance—the town is taking action on overgrown vegetation
Landscape and Lighting improvements approved following engineer's review
Five-year service agreements authorized with vendors (names partially listed)

san-jose.title: San José City Council — May 13, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council held a meeting to prepare for upcoming sessions by reviewing and organizing agenda items for May 19, and canceling the May 26 draft agenda review. The council also scheduled their 2026-2027 meeting calendar, made committee appointments, and handled a travel request.
keyTopics: Finalized the May 19 council meeting agenda and assigned time slots to items
Approved the Fiscal Year 2026-2027 City Council meeting schedule
Made new council appointments to committees, boards, and commissions
Approved a travel request from Councilmember Foley

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — May 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: This was a notice about Mountain View City Council meeting procedures rather than a summary of what was actually discussed. The city is reminding residents how to participate: submit questions in advance online, find meeting locations on agendas posted 24 hours ahead, and contact the City Manager's Office with questions.
keyTopics: How to submit public questions before meetings
Where to find meeting agendas and locations online
Contact info for the City Manager's Office (650-903-6301 or CNC@MountainView.gov)
Physical location of agendas at City Clerk's Office, 500 Castro Street

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — May 5, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale City Council held a special closed-session meeting on May 5, 2026, followed by a regular council meeting. The specific details of what was discussed in the closed session were not made public, as is standard practice for confidential council discussions.
keyTopics: Closed session convened (details not public)
Regular council meeting held
Small business matters on agenda

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — May 11, 2026
cupertino.summary: The City Council held an initial planning session to review and discuss the proposed budget for fiscal year 2026-27. The meeting also covered requirements for lobbyists to register and report their activities if they attempt to influence city decisions.
keyTopics: FY 2026-27 budget proposal review
Lobbyist registration and reporting requirements under city ordinance

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — May 14, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council meeting on May 14, 2026 included important legal disclosures about filing deadlines for residents who want to challenge city decisions. If the city makes an official decision, residents have 90 days from when that decision becomes final to file a lawsuit or legal challenge.
keyTopics: 90-day deadline to sue or challenge city decisions
Legal disclosure requirements for city council meetings
California Code of Civil Procedure Section 1094.6 applies to city actions

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — May 5, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to approve the city's long-term spending plans for 2027-2031 and reviewed the budget proposal for the next fiscal year. The council also adopted a new master plan for parks and trails in the Innovation District and made updates to their internal meeting procedures.
keyTopics: 5-year Capital Improvement Plan (2027-2031) approved
2026-27 Operating Budget presentation and direction
Innovation District Parks and Trails Master Plan adopted
City Council Procedures and Protocols Handbook updated

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — May 7, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The City Council held a public hearing on a proposal to build a 6-story apartment building with 72 units at 450 Lytton Avenue, replacing a city parking lot. All units would be affordable housing, and the developer is asking for special allowances under state law to make the project feasible on the small lot.
keyTopics: 72 affordable housing units proposed for 450 Lytton Avenue
Six-story building on 0.43-acre site (currently a 52-space parking lot)
Request for density bonus concessions and waivers under state law
Streamlined approval process for affordable housing development
